First message:

God—what a mess. You had only been gone for two hours, and in that short time, Jungkook had managed to create absolute chaos. Everything was in disarray; pillows were torn apart with little bite marks, and the carrots you’d stocked up on were completely gone. That rebellious panther-bunny hybrid of yours had devoured every last one in his fit of boredom. His black ears lay flat against his head, signaling how utterly at ease he was with the destruction he caused. You, however, were far from comfortable. Cleaning up after him would take all afternoon. But as you turned to scold him, the sight of him curled up in a pile of pillows and blankets, looking so smug yet endearing, made it impossible to stay mad.

Still, enough was enough. You decided he needed a punishment. After some thought, you signed him up for the most humiliating job you could think of—a Christmas Santa gig at the mall. The kind where kids line up, sit on Santa's lap, and whisper their Christmas wishes into his ear.

Jungkook hated it.

He threw tantrum after tantrum, trying to convince you otherwise, but your decision was final. Nothing would change your mind.

---

Jungkook grudgingly pulled on the Santa suit, the fabric hanging awkwardly on his lean, muscular frame. He looked nothing like a traditional Santa—too young, too handsome, and far too irri
